key_questions: Onset and duration? Associated with food/timing?
Possible causes: pregnancy (first trimester), viral gastroenteritis, medications, motion sickness, GERD, post-op?
Pregnancy test in women of reproductive age — mandatory
Signs of dehydration: dry mouth, decreased urine output, dizziness?
Recent food exposures (food poisoning), sick contacts (gastroenteritis)?
red_flags: Severe abdominal pain → appendicitis, bowel obstruction, pancreatitis — ER
Hematemesis (vomiting blood) or coffee-ground emesis → upper GI bleed — ER
Projectile vomiting (no nausea before) → central cause (raised ICP)
Signs of severe dehydration or electrolyte abnormality

first_line.name: Dimenhydrinate (Gravol)
first_line.drugs: Dimenhydrinate (Gravol) 25–50 mg q4–6h PRN | Dimenhydrinate 25–50 mg PO/IM | Gravol Ginger (natural health product)
first_line.notes: Dimenhydrinate: H1 antihistamine + anticholinergic — motion sickness and general nausea. OTC. Sedating (use with caution in elderly — falls; Beers Criteria). Avoid in BPH, glaucoma. Pregnancy: use cautiously (first trimester — prefer ginger or pyridoxine). Gravol Ginger: natural product, safe in pregnancy.
first_line.preferred_when: Motion sickness, mild-moderate nausea from various causes, short-term use

first_line.name: Diclectin (Pregnancy-specific)
first_line.drugs: Doxylamine 10 mg + pyridoxine (B6) 10 mg (Diclectin) — 2 tabs at bedtime, can increase to QID
first_line.notes: Health Canada approved for nausea/vomiting of pregnancy. First-line pharmacotherapy in pregnancy. Take 30 min before bed on empty stomach. Delayed-release formulation. Safe (extensive safety data).

second_line.name: Metoclopramide
second_line.notes: Dopamine antagonist + prokinetic. Effective for gastroparesis, chemotherapy nausea, post-op nausea. Avoid long-term (tardive dyskinesia risk). EPS risk — use short-term only.

second_line.name: Ondansetron
second_line.notes: 5-HT3 antagonist — highly effective for chemotherapy and post-op nausea. Increasing use for NVP (modest safety data, generally considered low risk after first trimester). QTc prolongation concern at high doses.

non_pharm: Oral rehydration: clear fluids in small sips (Pedialyte, diluted sports drink, clear broth)
BRAT diet (bland, easy to digest) once tolerating fluids: Bananas, Rice, Applesauce, Toast
Ginger (tea, ginger ale, ginger candies): evidence-based for nausea in pregnancy and chemotherapy
Small frequent meals; avoid spicy/fatty foods
P6 (Neiguan) acupressure wristbands: modest evidence for motion sickness and pregnancy nausea
Fresh air, avoid strong smells, stay hydrated
treatment.refer_when: Severe dehydration (unable to keep any fluids down); hematemesis; severe abdominal pain; pregnancy hyperemesis gravidarum (weight loss >5%, dehydration — requires IV fluids); nausea >48–72h without improvement
